_comment: Per-lever-family business-perspective templates used by app/skills/engine/business_detail.py to enrich initiatives with owner/accountability, KPIs, change-management, contract levers and a risk-mitigation library. Keyed by lever_family (supply|process|demand|technology|finance|structure) with a _default fallback. Deterministic so depth survives when the LLM is offline.

technology.owner_role: Chief Information Officer / IT Finance Lead

supply.comms_cadence: Bi-weekly steering committee; monthly CFO review
resistance_points: Business-unit preference for the status quo

process.comms_cadence: Weekly stand-ups during rollout; monthly governance review
resistance_points: Muscle memory of the legacy process

demand.comms_cadence: Monthly demand-board review; quarterly policy refresh

technology.comms_cadence: Bi-weekly FinOps / asset review; monthly steering

finance.comms_cadence: Monthly treasury review; quarterly supplier-terms council

structure.comms_cadence: Weekly transformation PMO; monthly executive and Board update

supply: Re-tender or competitively benchmark against should-cost before renewal
Negotiate volume-tiered pricing and rebate / growth incentives
Insert index-linked price clauses and a price-down ratchet
Consolidate volume to fewer suppliers for leverage
process: Standardize statements of work and service-level definitions
Embed continuous-improvement (productivity) clauses in service contracts
demand: Tighten catalog / policy controls and approval thresholds
Right-size specifications and demand volumes in contractual minimums
technology: Right-size licenses / seats at renewal and remove shelfware
Negotiate committed-use / enterprise agreements with true-down flexibility
Add usage-based pricing and FinOps cost-allocation terms
finance: Extend payment terms toward category / industry DPO benchmark
Negotiate early-payment discounts and dynamic-discounting options
Recover unclaimed rebates, input tax credits, and fee leakage
structure: Restructure leases and shared-service / outsourcing agreements
Align contracts to a consolidated operating-model footprint
_default: Review contract terms, renewal windows and pricing mechanisms before commitment

bounce_back_high.risk: Negotiated savings erode as prices, volumes or scope drift back over time
bounce_back_high.mitigation: Lock index-linked / ratchet clauses, set a savings-tracking baseline, and review realization monthly in the pipeline

bounce_back_medium.risk: Partial leakage of savings without active governance
bounce_back_medium.mitigation: Monthly run-rate tracking against the committed baseline with variance escalation

org_change_high.risk: High organizational change required; adoption and execution may stall
org_change_high.mitigation: Secure executive sponsor sign-off, stand up a dedicated PMO, and stage the rollout with clear decision gates

org_change_medium.risk: Moderate change management needed across affected teams
org_change_medium.mitigation: Assign change owners, communicate on a fixed cadence, and tie milestones to incentives

low_confidence.risk: Diagnosis rests on limited evidence; modeled savings may be over- or under-stated
low_confidence.mitigation: Run a targeted evidence probe (contracts, invoices, benchmarks) before committing at Gate 2

supplier_concentration.risk: High supplier concentration creates dependency and switching exposure
supplier_concentration.mitigation: Qualify a secondary source and dual-source critical lines before consolidating

_default.risk: Execution dependencies and data gaps could delay realization
_default.mitigation: Confirm condition precedents and required data fields before kickoff
